danger_signals.visual_form: Resource indicator UI depletion; unit state changes
danger_signals.meaning: Famine state when food depletes; units starve and die from lack of supplies

danger_signals.visual_form: Gold counter at zero or negative; unit status indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Unit mutiny against player; maintenance-requiring units defect to enemy control

danger_signals.visual_form: Resource counter depletion; ranged unit weapon status
danger_signals.meaning: Shooters and cannons cease fire; loss of ranged combat capability

danger_signals.visual_form: Isometric unit sprites moving on map; formation groupings
danger_signals.meaning: Military threat; potential imminent combat or resource raid

danger_signals.visual_form: Peasant unit sprite ownership change; color/allegiance shift
danger_signals.meaning: Economic workforce loss; enemy gains labor capacity

danger_signals.visual_form: Cannon/mortar fire trajectories; impact debris and shrapnel
danger_signals.meaning: Area-of-effect danger zone; collateral damage to nearby units (ally and enemy)

danger_signals.visual_form: Unit formation dissolution; soldiers breaking stance
danger_signals.meaning: Combat effectiveness reduction; units vulnerable to counter-attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Grouped unit clusters (15, 36, 72, 120, or 196 units); officer and drummer presence
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive bonus active; morale improvement; organized unit cohesion

safety_signals.visual_form: Unit stationary stance; formation maintained in defensive posture
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive bonus granted; morale improvement; reduced vulnerability to charge attacks

safety_signals.visual_form: Resource counters high; peasants working in designated areas (mills, mines, fishing boats)
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; no famine risk; unit maintenance sustainable
Reduced vulnerability to artillery; defended territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard units surrounding artillery; formation grouping
safety_signals.meaning: Protection against melee counter-attack; sustained ranged damage capability

faction_indicators.color_identity: Nation-specific color palette for unit sprites and building architecture
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Architectural style unique per nation (building visual distinctiveness)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy unit sprites rendered in different nation colors; building architecture reflects nation allegiance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Building styles cluster geographically; resource gathering sites tied to nation settlement patterns

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Can build xebecs (unique naval unit equivalent to frigates); restricted to galley-tier ships before xebec tech
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Coastal settlements; naval dominance capability; historically contextual positioning

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Access to full ship hierarchy (yacht, galley, frigate, battleship); advanced artillery (multi-barrelled cannons, howitzers)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Advanced military infrastructure; higher tech building availability

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Restricted unit build trees; limited to galley-tier naval units; reduced artillery access
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lower-tier building structures; reduced technological complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Resource indicator shows zero/negative food; peasants cease activity at mills and fishing boats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units die from starvation; economy collapses; workforce cannot expand

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold counter at zero; unit maintenance icons show alert status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gold-dependent units defect to enemy control; mutiny occurs; loss of expensive unit types (cavalry, artillery crew)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ranged unit weapon indicators show disabled state; cannon/shooter sprites show idle pose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shooters cannot fire volley; cannons and artillery become decorative structures; melee-only combat mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isometric unit formations visible on map; artillery fire trajectories traced; impact debris and shrapnel visual effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units assume combat formations; morale modifiers visible; wounded unit sprites or death animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Peasants visible gathering resources; no enemy units on screen; construction animation for buildings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resource gathering continues uninterrupted; technology research visible in academy/minaret; unit training ongoing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Building upgrade visual changes; unit sprite variants reflect armor/weapon upgrades; new unit types available in production menus
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Damage output increases for affected units; ranged units show improved accuracy/range; artillery reload speed increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ships visible in coastal/water areas; frigate/battleship sprites denote tech level; shore bombardment fire trajectories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Water territory controlled; enemy naval movement restricted; shore structures take damage from cannon fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unit formation dissolves visually; soldiers scatter sprite animation; officer/drummer units separated from formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units vulnerable to melee charge; combat effectiveness reduced; defensive bonus lost

environmental_storytelling.example: 16 playable nations each possess unique building styles; visual landscape communicates political/cultural identity of territory holder
Designated resource areas (gold mines, stone quarries, fishing zones, forests) concentrate around settlements; visual density indicates economic focus
Ship types (yacht → galley → frigate → battleship) visible in harbors reflect technological progression and military capability; xebec presence signals Turkish faction
Large grouped formations (up to 196 units) create visual mass on isometric map; player perceives army strength through physical cluster density
Building upgrades change architectural appearance; walls show reinforcement visuals; artillery-damaged structures display destruction states
Academy and minaret buildings require visible presence to enable formations; their absence communicates organizational chaos

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible at designated resource sites (mills, mines, quarries, fishing boats); multitasking capability shown through task-switching animation
Sprite color/allegiance changes; unit transitions to enemy control; player loses labor capacity
Maintains tight grouping; displays defensive posture; morale indicator shows elevated state
Dissolves from tight cluster; soldiers scatter; vulnerable to melee counter-attack; retreats backwards
Maintains distance from melee targets; positioned at range threshold for weapon effectiveness; volley animation triggers
Weapon disabled; unit assumes idle pose; cannot fire volleys; retreats when approached
Grouped movement; charge capability; faster movement than infantry; melee damage superiority over shooters
Fires projectile; arcing trajectory visible; impact debris and shrapnel create area-of-effect damage; friendly fire possible if unit caught in blast radius
Weapon disabled; unit becomes immobile defensive structure; mutiny risk if gold also depleted
Maintains crew loyalty; active naval combat; can bombard shore structures; controls water territory
Crew mutiny; ship captured by enemy or becomes uncontrollable; crew defects with vessel
Enables formation assembly (15/36/72/120/196 unit groupings); grants morale bonus; allows stand ground order effectiveness
Formation capability lost; units revert to solo attack mode; morale penalty applied; defensive bonus unavailable

notes: Cossacks: European Wars communicates world-state primarily through resource depletion mechanics manifested in unit behavioral changes and UI indicators. The 16-nation faction system uses architectural distinctiveness rather than explicit color identity as the primary visual differentiation. Danger signals are encoded in resource scarcity (famine, mutiny, weapon malfunction) and unit formation dissolution (morale loss). Safety is signaled through formation maintenance, officer presence, and stand ground postures. The isometric perspective allows visual spectacle of large unit clusters, making army strength physically perceivable. Environmental storytelling relies on building-style clustering and resource location patterns. Unit behavioral tells are tightly coupled to resource state (coal/iron shortage disables weapons; gold shortage triggers mutiny). The game's political situation is implicit in historical mission scenarios (Thirty Years' War, War of Austrian Succession) rather than explicit world-state visualization.
